Capture_Input.Notes: Comment in src/main.cpp mentions GPIO16; confirm wiring.

Display.Interface: Arduino_GFX (RGB panel) via Dev Device Pins

UI_EVT_STATUS_SYNC.Purpose: Keep arc/dropdown synced if backend changes state.

Current_Status.Phase: UI bring-up: orientation, layout, touch alignment
Findings: Display is showing portrait because gfx->setRotation uses a GT911 rotation constant; display rotation and touch rotation are likely mismatched.
Arc and dropdown overlap due to mid-screen alignment; layout needs top-left and top-right anchors for landscape.
Arc drag and dropdown selection are inconsistent, indicating touch coordinates are not mapped to display coordinates.
lv_indev_set_display is not called; input device may not be tied to the active display.
Conflicts: Rotation constants for GT911 (ROTATION_*) are not guaranteed to match Arduino_GFX rotation values.
Pending_Tasks: Set landscape rotation for display and touch using explicit rotation constants per library, and update GT911 resolution to match the rotated display.
Bind the LVGL input device to the active display with lv_indev_set_display.
Re-layout arc and dropdown to top-left/top-right for landscape with explicit margins.
Verify arc direction (right increases, left decreases) and adjust arc mode/angles if needed.
Ensure dropdown selection uses correct touch mapping after rotation changes.
Notes: Touch pins from reference: SDA=8, SCL=4, INT=3, RST=38.
UI must clamp RPM to 100..6000 and map dropdown index 0..3 to patternFromIndex().
Current symptoms: portrait orientation, overlap, arc drag at random points, dropdown selection not registering.
